arts.ttl: Vincent Kompany delighted with Bayern Munich’s 1-1 draw with Celtic, all things considered
arts.sum: They made incredibly hard work of it, but thanks to a late, stoppage time goal from substitute Alphonso Davies, Bayern Munich drew Celtic 1-1 at the Allianz Arena and went through 3-2 on aggregate in the Champions League playoff round. The two legs against Celtic might not have been anywhere near Bayern at their best, but they are still top of the Bundesliga and on to the next round of the Champions League after a tough stretch of fixtures.

arts.ttl: Nicolas Kühn almost sunk old team during Celtic’s 1-1 draw with Bayern Munich in the Champions League
arts.sum: Kühn almost scored in the first leg and also had another chance earlier in the match, but the 25-year-old finally struck gold in the 63rd minute to put Celtic up 1-0 — and put Bayern Munich firmly in danger of elimination of the Champions League. If there was any player with motivation to help Celtic FC take down Bayern Munich in the Champions League, it was former Bayern prospect Nicolas Kühn.

arts.ttl: ‘They gave everything’: Rodgers proud of Celtic after late defeat by Bayern
arts.athr: Ewan Murray at the Allianz Arena
arts.sum: Stoppage-time Alphonso Davies goal settles playoff ‘I can only take immense pride from the performance’ Brendan Rodgers expressed his “immense pride” at Celtic’s showing at the Allianz Arena, after a goal in stoppage time by Alphonso Davies denied the Scottish champions extra time in their Champions League playoff against Bayern Munich. Celtic led the second leg of their playoff tie 1-0 after Nicolas Kühn’s 63rd-minute goal and caused their hosts all manner of problems. Bayern scraped through 3-2 on aggregate but Celtic took the plaudits. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Rodgers aims to make Celtic 'seasoned club at this level'
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers hails his Celtic players after their Champions League journey ends despite a 1-1 draw with Bayern Munich in Germany.

arts.ttl: 'Unswerving plan is to make Celtic seasoned club at this level'
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers hails his Celtic players after their Champions League journey ends despite a 1-1 draw with Bayern Munich in Germany.

arts.ttl: Three Observations from Bayern Munich’s lackluster 1-1 draw against Celtic in the Champions League
arts.sum: Bayern Munich looked like they were on course for February elimination, the first since losing to Liverpool in 2019, after going 1-0 down to Celtic with half an hour to go. See Joshua Kimmich’s left footed shot, Serge Gnabry’s early chance, and Harry Kane hitting the crossbar (it’s come back to haunt the team again) just before half-time.

arts.ttl: Bavarian Podcast Works — Postgame Show: Bayern Munich 1-1 Celtic (Champions League)
arts.sum: Also, be sure to stay tuned to Bavarian Podcast Works for all of your up to date coverage on Bayern Munich and Germany. Only a last minute goal from Alphonso Davies — yup, he dressed for the match — saved Bayern Munich from having to go to extra time facing a Celtic side that was hungry to send the Germans out of the competition.

arts.ttl: Match Awards from Bayern Munich’s close call 1-1 draw vs. Celtic in the Champions League
arts.sum: Bayern Munich needed to deliver their best performance today to advance in the Champions League, and while they accomplished that mission, it was a struggle to do so. Bayern’s midfield powerhouse finding his form again, Goretzka brought a mix of physicality and late attacking attempts that made him a constant threat from even the midfield.

arts.ttl: ‘Will stand us in good stead for the future’: Schmeichel on Celtic's UCL experience
arts.sum: ‘It will stand us in good stead for the future’: Kasper Schmeichel on Celtic's UCL experience

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich effort gives Celtic European credibility – Brendan Rodgers
arts.sum: The German champions needed Alphonso Davies’ stoppage-time goal to win the Champions League tie.
